name: Dr Matteo Redana
focus: Computer vision • Machine learning for remote sensing • Tropical ecosystem monitoring
bio: Matteo Redana is a researcher at Northern Arizona University specializing in remote sensing, computer vision, and environmental data analysis. His work focuses on applying advanced computational methods to study ecosystem dynamics, particularly in tropical habitats, using satellite imagery and thermal data. Redana integrates machine learning and time-lagged modeling approaches to better understand spatial and temporal environmental patterns. His research contributes to improving the accuracy of ecosystem monitoring and supports efforts to analyze how landscapes respond to climate and environmental change.
